name: Cow milk, pasteurized, vitamin A + D added, 0% fat / nonfat skim (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fluid milk (approx. per 100 mL; density ~1.033 g/mL)

components.name: lactose
components.source: USDA FDC 168935 (total sugars 5.09 g/100g, ~all lactose in skim milk); Fox & McSweeney, Dairy Chemistry & Biochemistry 4th ed. 2015
components.evidence: DOMINANT carbon/energy source. Total sugars in this item = 5.09 g/100g; in bovine milk essentially 100% is the disaccharide lactose (beta-D-galactosyl-(1->4)-D-glucose). MW 342.3.

components.name: D-galactose (free)
components.source: Fox & McSweeney 2015; USDA lists it within total sugars
components.evidence: Only trace free galactose in fresh unfermented milk (<20-50 mg/100g); the bulk of galactose is bound in lactose. Present in current list.

components.name: D-glucose (free)
components.evidence: Trace free glucose in fresh milk (~5-20 mg/100g); most glucose is bound in lactose. Present in current list.

components.name: citric acid (citrate)
components.source: Fox & McSweeney, Dairy Chemistry & Biochemistry 2015; Belitz & Grosch, Food Chemistry (1.75 g/L typical, range 1.32-2.1 g/L)
components.evidence: MISSING from the current FooDB list and it is THE major organic acid of milk (~1.75 g/L). Growth-relevant carbon source and Ca/Mg chelator. KEY ADDITION.

components.name: orotic acid (orotate)
components.source: Larson & Hegarty; Urbiene & Leskauskaite 2006 Pol.J.Food Nutr.Sci. 15/56(3):277-281 (nucleic-acid/orotate in raw milk); typical ~50-80 mg/L
components.evidence: Milk is a characteristic dietary orotate source (pyrimidine biosynthesis intermediate); consumed as a growth factor by some lactic acid bacteria. MISSING; added.

components.name: L-lactic acid (L-lactate)
components.evidence: Low in FRESH/pasteurized milk (<0.01-0.05 g/100g); rises sharply only on fermentation/spoilage. Present in current list; amount set to fresh-milk trace level.

components.name: pyruvic acid (pyruvate)
components.evidence: Trace TCA-cycle intermediate; present in current list.

components.name: succinic acid (succinate)
components.evidence: Trace organic acid; present in current list.

components.name: L-malic acid (L-malate)
components.evidence: Minor TCA intermediate in milk; present in current list.

components.name: fumaric acid (fumarate)
components.evidence: Trace organic acid; present in current list.

components.name: hippuric acid
components.source: raw-milk HPLC organic-acid profile (hippuric ~3.6 mg/L); Horak et al. 1996
components.evidence: Trace; converted to benzoic acid by lactic acid bacteria during fermentation.

components.name: urea
components.source: Fox & McSweeney 2015 (milk urea nitrogen ~10-14 mg/dL -> urea ~250 mg/L)
components.evidence: Largest single non-protein-nitrogen fraction of milk; usable N source. Present in current list.

components.name: protein (total, casein+whey)
components.evidence: Predominantly caseins (~80%) + whey proteins (~20%); provides the amino acids below on hydrolysis.

components.name: L-glutamic acid
components.evidence: Protein-bound total; most abundant amino acid in milk protein.

components.name: L-proline
components.evidence: Protein-bound total.

components.name: L-leucine
components.evidence: Protein-bound total; essential.

components.name: L-aspartic acid
components.evidence: Protein-bound total.

components.name: L-lysine
components.evidence: Protein-bound total; essential.

components.name: L-valine
components.evidence: Protein-bound total; essential.

components.name: L-serine
components.evidence: Protein-bound total. (Replaces the FooDB 'D-Serine' artifact.)

components.name: L-phenylalanine
components.evidence: Protein-bound total; essential.

components.name: L-isoleucine
components.evidence: Protein-bound total; essential.

components.name: L-tyrosine
components.evidence: Protein-bound total.

components.name: L-threonine
components.evidence: Protein-bound total; essential.

components.name: L-alanine
components.evidence: Protein-bound total.

components.name: L-histidine
components.evidence: Protein-bound total; essential.

components.name: L-arginine
components.evidence: Protein-bound total.

components.name: L-methionine
components.evidence: Protein-bound total; essential, sulfur source.

components.name: glycine
components.evidence: Protein-bound total.

components.name: L-tryptophan
components.evidence: Protein-bound total; essential.

components.name: L-cystine (cysteine)
components.evidence: Protein-bound total; sulfur amino acid.

components.name: calcium
components.evidence: Largely colloidal (casein-micelle phosphate) + soluble; essential cation.

components.name: phosphorus (phosphate)
components.evidence: Inorganic + organic (casein) phosphate; P source. Current 'Phosphate' entry.

components.name: potassium
components.evidence: Major soluble cation.

components.name: chloride
components.source: Fox & McSweeney 2015 (milk chloride ~1.0 g/L); not in USDA panel
components.evidence: Major milk anion (~90-110 mg/100g); present in current list.

components.name: sodium
components.evidence: Soluble cation.

components.name: magnesium
components.evidence: Essential divalent cation.

components.name: sulfate / sulfur
components.evidence: Sulfur supplied mainly via Met/Cys and inorganic sulfate (~10 mg/100g); present in current list, amount unquantified here.

components.name: zinc
components.evidence: Trace element; casein-associated.

components.name: iron
components.evidence: Milk is iron-poor. Consolidates the three FooDB iron/redox/compartment entries ('Iron', 'Iron (Fe3+)', 'Fe2+ mitochondria') into one real value.

components.name: copper
components.evidence: Ultratrace element.

components.name: manganese
components.evidence: Ultratrace element.

components.name: selenium
components.evidence: Ultratrace; largely selenoprotein/selenomethionine bound.

components.name: fluoride
components.evidence: Ultratrace; present in current list.

components.name: molybdenum (molybdate)
components.evidence: Genuine ultratrace essential element; present in current list. Amount unquantified in USDA panel.

components.name: chromium
components.evidence: Genuine ultratrace element in milk; present in current list.

components.name: thiamin (B1)
components.evidence: Water-soluble vitamin.

components.name: riboflavin (B2)
components.evidence: Milk is a major dietary riboflavin source.

components.name: niacin (B3, nicotinate)
components.evidence: Present in current list as 'Nicotinate'.

components.name: pantothenic acid (B5)
components.evidence: Water-soluble vitamin.

components.name: pyridoxine (B6)
components.evidence: Present in current list.

components.name: folate (B9)
components.evidence: Present in current list as 'Folate'.

components.name: cobalamin (B12)
components.evidence: Present in current list as 'Cobalamin'; carries the milk's biologically relevant cobalt.

components.name: vitamin C (ascorbate)
components.evidence: USDA reports 0 for this item (largely lost on pasteurization).

components.name: vitamin A (retinol, added)
components.evidence: Fortified (added vitamin A). Present as 'Retinol'.

components.name: vitamin D (added)
components.evidence: Fortified (added vitamin D). Present as 'Vitamin D'.

components.name: vitamin E (alpha-tocopherol)
components.evidence: Near-zero in 0% fat skim milk (fat-soluble, removed with fat). Present in current list.

components.name: vitamin K (phylloquinone)
components.evidence: Fat-soluble; ~0 in skim milk. Present as 'Phytomenadione'.

components.name: choline
components.evidence: Present in current list.

components.name: myo-inositol
components.evidence: Genuine minor milk component; present in current list.

components.name: L-carnitine
components.evidence: Genuine minor milk metabolite; present in current list.

components.name: taurine
components.evidence: Minor free amino sulfonic acid; present in current list.

components.name: glycine betaine
components.evidence: Osmolyte / methyl donor; genuine minor milk component; present in current list.

components.name: creatine
components.evidence: Genuine minor non-protein-nitrogen metabolite; present in current list (and uncovered).

components.name: cholesterol
components.evidence: Only ~2 mg/100g in 0% fat skim milk (fat mostly removed); genuine for an animal food (not implausible here), retained as trace.

remove.name: Arsenate
remove.reason: Non-nutritive toxic trace contaminant; not a growth nutrient of milk.

remove.name: Cadmium
remove.reason: Non-nutritive heavy-metal contaminant.

remove.name: Aluminum
remove.reason: Non-nutritive trace contaminant; not an essential nutrient.

remove.name: Rubidium
remove.reason: Non-nutritive trace element; incidental, not a growth nutrient.

remove.name: Boron
remove.reason: Non-essential ultratrace element; not a defining milk growth nutrient.

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: Non-essential ultratrace contaminant for this medium.

remove.name: Methanol
remove.reason: Trace volatile / FooDB artifact; not a milk growth nutrient.

remove.name: Acetone
remove.reason: Trace volatile ketone (blood-derived); not a growth nutrient at milk levels.

remove.name: D-Ornithine
remove.reason: D-enantiomer is a FooDB stereochemistry artifact; milk ornithine is the L-form and only trace.

remove.name: D-Serine
remove.reason: D-enantiomer is a FooDB artifact; replaced by protein-derived L-serine.

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+)
remove.reason: Redundant redox-state duplicate of iron; consolidated into single 'iron' entry.

remove.name: Fe2+ mitochondria
remove.reason: FooDB subcellular-compartment artifact; consolidated into single 'iron' entry.

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: Free cobalt ion is a FooDB artifact; milk's biologically relevant cobalt is delivered as cobalamin (B12), which is retained.

remove.name: L Kynurenine C10H12N2O3
remove.reason: Trace tryptophan-catabolism metabolite; not a growth-relevant milk nutrient.

remove.name: Butyryl carnitine
remove.reason: Trace acylcarnitine metabolite; not a defined growth nutrient (free L-carnitine retained).

remove.name: Propionyl-carnitine
remove.reason: Trace acylcarnitine metabolite; not a defined growth nutrient.

remove.name: Tetradecanoyl carnitine
remove.reason: Trace acylcarnitine metabolite; not a defined growth nutrient.

remove.name: L-Acetylcarnitine
remove.reason: Trace acylcarnitine metabolite; not a defined growth nutrient.

remove.name: N-acetyl-L-cysteine
remove.reason: Not a genuine free milk constituent at nutritional level; FooDB noise.

remove.name: 3-Methylbutanoic acid
remove.reason: Trace flavor volatile; not a growth nutrient.

remove.name: D-Galactarate
remove.reason: Implausible/predicted oxidation product; not a measured milk nutrient.

missing_added: citric acid (citrate) ~0.175 g/100g -- THE major organic acid of milk, absent from the FooDB list (key growth-relevant carbon source and Ca/Mg chelator)
orotic acid (orotate) ~7 mg/100g -- characteristic milk pyrimidine metabolite / LAB growth factor
explicit lactose amount (~4.9 g/100g) -- dominant carbon/energy source, quantified
chloride (~100 mg/100g) and quantified mineral/vitamin panel
keep_uncovered: PC(...) diacyl/plasmalogen phosphatidylcholines and SM(d18:0/16:0) -- genuine milk-fat-globule-membrane phospholipids, but this is 0% FAT skim milk so they are removed with the fat and are near-negligible; no BiGG metabolite anyway
Levoglutamide (glutamine), 2-Oxopentanedioic acid (2-oxoglutarate), Propanedioic acid (malonate) -- genuine trace metabolites without clean growth-nutrient amounts
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 168935 -- Milk, nonfat, fluid, with added vitamin A and vitamin D (fat free or skim): full proximate/mineral/vitamin/amino-acid panel per 100 g
Fox, McSweeney, Cogan & Guinee, Dairy Chemistry and Biochemistry, 4th ed., Springer 2015 -- citrate (~1.75 g/L), chloride, minor sugars, minerals, carnitine, inositol
Belitz, Grosch & Schieberle, Food Chemistry -- milk organic-acid profile (citric as major acid)
Urbiene S. & Leskauskaite D. 2006, Pol. J. Food Nutr. Sci. 15/56(3):277-281 -- milk organic/nucleic acids incl. orotate and hippurate

Reversed-phase HPLC dairy organic-acid profiles (Lee et al. 1995 Korean J. Dairy Sci.; Fernandez-Garcia & McGregor 1994 J. Dairy Sci. 77:2934) -- lactate/pyruvate/succinate/hippurate/orotate quantities
notes: Dominant carbon/energy source is LACTOSE (~4.9 g/100g, ~145 mM); the second, growth-relevant carbon source is CITRATE (~0.175 g/100g, ~9 mM) which was entirely missing from the FooDB list. Nitrogen is supplied by milk protein/amino acids (3.37 g/100g protein, casein-dominant) plus ~0.025 g/100g urea. Because this item is 0% fat, fat-soluble vitamins (E, K) and the MFGM phospholipids (PC/SM species) are near-zero. Removed FooDB noise: toxic/non-nutritive trace metals (arsenate, cadmium, aluminium, rubidium, boron, nickel), free-ion and subcellular-compartment artifacts (Co2+, Fe2+ mitochondria, Iron Fe3+), D-enantiomer artifacts (D-serine, D-ornithine), and trace volatiles/acylcarnitines.
